statement: Under the UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement (2020), the UK regained the status of an independent coastal state with sovereign rights over its EEZ. The agreement included a 5.5-year transition period (2021–2026) with EU fishing access gradually reduced by 25%. Post-transition, annual negotiations determine mutual access and quota sharing.
source_name: UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement, Part Two, Heading Five
caveats: The UK fisheries outcome post-Brexit has been controversial, with many UK fishers arguing the 25% reduction in EU access was insufficient. This provides a cautionary precedent for both sides of Iceland's debate.

related_entries.statement: The UK voted to leave the EU in June 2016 (51.9% Leave) and formally departed on 31 January 2020. The UK Office for Budget Responsibility estimated in 2023 that Brexit would reduce long-run UK GDP by approximately 4% compared to remaining in the EU, primarily through reduced trade intensity.

claims.claim_text: Hvert aðildarríki gefur út, skipuleggur og afturkallar veiðileyfi, samkvæmt reglugerð ESB nr. 1224/2009, grein 6(5).
Skilyrðin fyrir úthlutun veiðiheimilda eru að skip verði að vera undir fána aðildarríkisins og úthlutunin verði að vera gagnsæ og hlutlæg.
